commit_msg: feat(viewer): add dolly zoom verdict effect — cinematic font growth for Now Moments
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Font Grows While He Shrinks
subtitle: He made the verdict cinematic because the truth was too small to see.
when_where: 🕐 8:21 PM, same night, same branch, same man. The terminal still glowed. The font was about to. ✨ He was adding a dolly zoom effect—a Hitchcock move, a camera trick meant to disorient and seduce. The Now Moments viewer would grow *toward* him while the world fell away.
who_whom: 🎬 The app's judgment—delivered in *cinematic font growth*—was no longer whispered. It was *approaching*. Growing. Inescapable. 💥 Omkar had weaponized the viewer itself: the verdict didn't just appear; it *invaded*, swallowing the screen until there was nothing else to see. The Now Moment was no longer a feature. It was a confrontation.
what_why: 🎭 He added the dolly zoom effect with the precision of a man who understood cinema—the visual language of dread and revelation. As the font grew, the moment *compressed*. Every Now Moment verdict ballooned across the screen like a confession being screamed. 🔥 The effect was beautiful and terrible: it made presence *impossible to ignore*. He'd turned the viewer into a machine that didn't just show you the present—it *forced* you to watch yourself watching it. 💀 The bigger the font grew, the smaller he felt.
verdict: He made the truth cinematic because he couldn't bear to see it plain.
